⚡ Bolt: Resolve N+1 query bottleneck in Search Service (#115)

* perf: resolve N+1 query in molecule tag enrichment

Fixed an N+1 query bottleneck inside `enrichAtomsWithMoleculeTags` in `engine/src/services/search/search.ts` by replacing the per-compound loop query with a batched `SELECT ... WHERE compound_id = ANY($1)` query. Added journaling for this performance learning.

commit.message:
ci: unified concurrency group for CI1→CI2→CD1 pipeline

All three automated stages share deploy-pipeline-$ref so a new
push to main cancels any in-progress run and restarts from CI1.
PR runs stay isolated in their own group. CD2/CD3 keep separate
groups (queue, don't cancel — manual deploys are intentional).

fix(ui): fully reset composer on new thread / delete all / refresh

Added resetComposer() helper that clears:
- textarea content + auto-height reset
- search mode dropdown back to 'auto'
- file input selection

- status bar

Called from newChat(), purgeAllData(), and initializeApp() so no
stale state survives across sessions without needing F5.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: improve SLM entity resolution, name search, and output formatting (#1350)

The admin chat / SLM was effectively useless for natural language queries:
- 'show customer X' failed because 'customer' didn't match slug 'customers'
- Name-based lookups were impossible — only numeric IDs accepted
- Record output was just ToString() with no field data

Changes:
- Add fuzzy entity resolution (singular/plural, prefix, contains matching)
  in both AdminToolCatalogue (Intelligence) and QueryTools (AI)
- Add 'show-entity' intent + handler that loads by ID or searches by name
- Add SearchByName AI tool for the Copilot/IChatClient path
- Extract search text from natural language queries (everything after
  the entity name becomes a 'query' parameter)
- Format record output with actual field values instead of ToString()
- Improve help text to document new capabilities

Fixes #1338

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs(*): add bootstrap step to `copilot-instructions.md` (#517)

The Copilot instructions did not tell agents to bootstrap the workspace
before running validation or making package changes. In a fresh clone,
that leads to avoidable failures and wasted setup time.

- **Summary**
- Add explicit bootstrap guidance to `.github/copilot-instructions.md`.
- Place it directly before the existing test/build/lint workflow so the
install requirement is seen before any validation commands.

- **Changes**
  - Introduce a new `Bootstrapping` section in the Copilot instructions.
- Require `npm install` from the repo root before tests, builds, lint,
or implementation work.
- Clarify that work should not start until install completes
successfully because workspace dependencies are shared across the
monorepo.

- **Resulting guidance**
  ```md
  ## Bootstrapping

- In a fresh clone, run `npm install` from the repo root before
attempting tests, builds, lint, or package changes.
- Do not start validation or implementation work until the install has
completed successfully, because the workspace dependencies are required
across the monorepo.
  ```

Integrate DWPose whole-body pose conditioning into full pipeline

- Shard creation: include pose.npy as required (missing = skip record)
- Data loader: load pose.npy (133,3) float16 → float32, stack in collate_fn

  null_pose parameter, confidence masking, CFG dropout via cfg_drop_pose

  5% DINO-CLS-only, 5% DINO-patches-only, 10% drop-pose, 5% pose-only)
- Config: num_pose_joints=133, pose_confidence_threshold=0.05,
  p_drop_pose=0.10, p_pose_only=0.05

publish
- Publish is conditional on actual version bump (`has_bump`), so pushes
with no pending changes skip publishing
- CI becomes PR-only since CD handles main
- Block `major` version bumps in CI (we're not ready for 1.0.0)

## Infinite loop prevention
The version bump commit message includes `[skip ci]`, which GitHub
Actions natively respects to skip all workflows.

## Test plan
- [ ] Merge a PR with a `patch` change file → CD bumps version →
publishes → `[skip ci]` commit doesn't re-trigger
- [ ] Merge a PR with no change files → CD builds/tests but skips
publish
- [ ] PR with a `major` change file → CI blocks it before merge
- [ ] CI no longer runs on push to main

commit.message:
fix: delegate runtime actions to CommandService in DataCommandHandler (#1346)

Runtime-defined entities (e.g. the todo sample) register actions with
Method=null in RemoteCommandMetadata. DataCommandHandler previously
attempted to invoke this null MethodInfo via reflection, throwing a
NullReferenceException that was silently swallowed by a bare catch.

Now when cmd.Method is null the handler delegates to CommandService
which properly resolves the RuntimeActionModel, expands commands via
the ActionExpander, and applies field mutations.

Also surfaces exception messages in both DataCommandHandler and
ActionApiHandlers catch blocks instead of generic 'internal error'.

Fixes #1337

* Fix MentionInput parser regression

Repair the malformed merge artifact in MentionInput that was breaking frontend lint in PR 2651.

The failing GitHub Actions run reported a parser error in frontend/src/components/chat/MentionInput.tsx. The file had duplicated fragments from two versions of the component: an imperative handle block had been spliced into the mount effect, the keydown callback was missing its closing brace before the dependency array, and the placeholder JSX tail contained both the newer responsive placeholder path and an older duplicate fallback block.

This change removes the duplicated fragments and restores the intended component structure without altering runtime behavior. The final file keeps the existing responsive placeholder support, cycling placeholder rendering, and imperative handle API, but returns to a syntactically valid single implementation.

Validation: ran frontend lint locally with npm run lint in the PR 2651 worktree, and eslint completed successfully after the repair.
